answer: Monitor hand movement and sensation throughout; be ready to untie quickly or cut the ropes in a controlled way if warning signs appear or an emergency occurs.
evidence:
Hand sensations and movement (closing and opening hands) should be continuously monitored to ensure everything is in working order. Good placement of the single-column tie and chest wrap will contribute to reduce the risks.
Again, these tips can only help mitigate the risks. Continuously monitor hand movement and sensation while the tie is on. A common tip is to scratch the palms and the top of the thumb to monitor the first signs of sensation loss. Be ready to untie quickly and cut the ropes with control in case of emergency. Fell free to skip ahead and read more about nerves in Day 50.
